description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who must enforce a lightweight **“policy-as-code”** rule that every source-code repository must have a disk-usage log that can be consumed by automated compliance tooling.

  1.  The source repository you must inspect is located at  
      /home/user/policy-code  

      It already contains some files.  **Do NOT modify or remove anything inside that directory.**

  2.  Create the directory  
      /home/user/reports  
      if it does not already exist.  All reports for this policy must live here.

  3.  Generate a one-off disk-usage report for /home/user/policy-code and write it to  
      /home/user/reports/policy_disk_usage.log

      The report **must contain exactly three lines, each terminated with a single UNIX newline (`\n`) and nothing else**.  
      The required format (including case, ordering and the “`=`” delimiter) is:

      DIRECTORY=/home/user/policy-code  
      TOTAL_FILES=<integer count of regular files under /home/user/policy-code>  
      TOTAL_BYTES=<integer sum of the file sizes in bytes under /home/user/policy-code>

      •  Use only regular files in your counts (ignore directories, symlinks, sockets, etc.).  
      •  The values must reflect the current state on disk at the moment you run your commands.  
      •  Do not add blank lines, comments or extra spaces.  
      •  Make sure the file mode of /home/user/reports/policy_disk_usage.log is 0644 (rw-r--r--).

  4.  Keep the solution short and transparent: the entire task can be completed with two or three standard GNU/Linux commands (e.g., a single `mkdir`, one pipeline for counting files, and one pipeline for counting bytes).  Feel free to echo or redirect your results directly into the log file.

  5.  When you are done, list the contents of /home/user/reports so that a reviewer can see the newly created log file (this is purely for human visibility; the automated grader will open the file to verify its exact contents).

  The automated test suite will:
  •  Check that /home/user/reports/policy_disk_usage.log exists and is world-readable.  
  •  Parse the three key-value pairs and verify that the integers match the actual number of regular files and their cumulative byte size under /home/user/policy-code at grading time.  
  •  Fail if extra lines, extra whitespace, or different key names are present.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing directory tree that the automated tests rely upon.
    The agent must NOT alter these files; only read their metadata.

    /home/user/policy-code/
    ├── policy.yaml         (113 bytes)
    ├── README.md           (88 bytes)
    ├── script.sh           (67 bytes)
    └── tests
        └── test1.txt       (16 bytes)

    Exact ASCII contents:

    /home/user/policy-code/policy.yaml
    ------------------------------------------------
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Policy
    metadata:
      name: sample-policy
    spec:
      description: Sample security policy file.
    ------------------------------------------------

    /home/user/policy-code/README.md
    ------------------------------------------------
    # Policy Code

    This repository contains sample policy code used for disk usage testing.
    ------------------------------------------------

    /home/user/policy-code/script.sh
    ------------------------------------------------
    #!/bin/bash
    # Sample enforcement script
    echo "Enforcing policy..."
    ------------------------------------------------

    /home/user/policy-code/tests/test1.txt
    ------------------------------------------------
    dummy test file
    ------------------------------------------------

    Ground-truth metrics that the grader will verify:

      EXPECTED_TOTAL_FILES  = 4
      EXPECTED_TOTAL_BYTES  = 284

    After the agent finishes, the file
      /home/user/reports/policy_disk_usage.log
    must exist with *exactly* the following byte sequence
    (each \n shown below is a single 0x0A newline character):

      DIRECTORY=/home/user/policy-code\n
      TOTAL_FILES=4\n
      TOTAL_BYTES=284\n

    The directory /home/user/reports must have permissions 0755
    and the log file itself 0644.  The grader will fail the task
    if the file contents deviate in any way.
  -->
name: task_000000_2b0645d6
